title: silene oculata
intro: Silene oculata, also known as blind lady's earring or moonwort, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues. Silene oculata has been reported to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and even exhibiting antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, or dried extracts, which are often used to make teas, salves, or poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
